name: ptouch-cradle
description: Two-part desktop cradle + label-catch tray for Brother PT-P750W label printer. Round 2: back panel raised to 205mm so the owl face lives ABOVE the installed printer (face zone z=143-205, the only zone visible in actual use). Owl face: heart-shaped (rounded-shield) facial disc carved into the convex panel face, recessed eye sockets (NOT proud domes), no pupils, asymmetric hooked beak. Ear tufts rebuilt from scratch as splayed feather clumps (3 hulled feather profiles per tuft) with outward-only lean — no forward sweep, no curl. New host-object proxy module renders the printer in place for use-state heroes.

params.cable_slot_style: top-open notch (no bridge), resides entirely within low perimeter wall zone z=0-25mm. Lives on user-back (-Y) face.
params.render_with_host_note: Top-level OpenSCAD parameter render_with_host (default false). When true, host_object_proxy() draws a 78x152x143 box at the printer's installed position inside the pocket. STL exports MUST keep render_with_host=false (proxy excluded from print geometry); use-state PNG renders override via --param render_with_host=true.
params.face_beak_shape: asymmetric hooked raptor beak — narrow at top, widening to base, tip pointing down with slight forward hook curl
params.tuft_silhouette: splayed feather clump — 3 hulled feather profiles per tuft, outward lean only (no forward curl, no Y-direction tilt), round-tipped, base blends into rounded panel top with r=4 fillet
params.label_drop_note: PT-P750W auto-cuts tape; labels exit at z=64-79mm desk-ref, clear front wall top at z=29mm desk-ref by 35-50mm, drop forward and down into tray.
params.tape_exit_position: lower-middle of front face, confirmed from product photography
params.usb_dc_ports_face: back (assumed, not confirmed in product photos)
params.user_orientation_note: +Y = user-front (tray slides out here; owl face visible from here). -Y = user-back (against-wall, cable notch). Render in user-frame per id/brief.md hero_views. PRIMARY hero is the use-state render with the printer proxy installed — silhouette test runs on cradle-user-front-in-use.png.

testPrintCandidates.reason: Tray exterior (103.2 mm W × 94.2 mm D × 21.6 mm H) must slide smoothly into cradle slot with 0.35 mm per-side clearance. If FDM dimensional error skews either part, tray will bind or rattle.

testPrintCandidates.feature: tray scoop angle with integrated finger-grip
testPrintCandidates.reason: Scoop face at 45° from horizontal plus a central concave finger-grip dip (2.5mm × 30mm) prints as threshold overhang. Verify slicer does not add unnecessary support and surface quality reads cleanly on the scoop + grip combined feature.

Each tuft = 3 hulled feather profiles per side, leaning 10/20/30° outward, no forward sweep. Per-feather lean angles are all <= 30° from vertical so supports should be unnecessary, but verify the hull does not produce a transient underhang. Round-1 forward-sweep tufts are dead and must not return.
3mm sagitta over 86mm chord (round 2 bumped from 2mm). Verify the convexity reads as a deliberate hug-the-printer curve rather than a manufacturing defect or an unwanted bulge.

Heart-shaped recessed disc with 16x20 mm eye recesses (1.5mm depth). The face must read as a real-owl face (not cartoon mascot) AND must be visible above the printer in the use-state render. If the use-state silhouette test fails, the round failed.
Tray walls at 1.6 mm (4 perimeters at 0.4 mm) are above minimum but thin. Verify wall integrity.
